sections.name: QA Process
sections.content:
Welcome

Welcome to the buildee team! This guide will help you get started and provide essential resources to begin working effectively as part of the QA team.
Production app.buildee.comLive user-facing environment.
QA qa.buildee.com
Auto-deployed when code merges into the develop branch
Stagingstaging.buildee.com
Pre-production validation
Shares DB/users with prod
Most stable, hosts automation runs
Default test envQA

Tools Overview

JIRA https://simuwatt.atlassian.net/
Slack Team communication & CI notifications
Postman API testing
Playwright E2E browser automation framework

QA Workflow in Jira

QA validates all tickets in "Ready for QA" status. 

Once testing has passed, the ticket should be moved to ✅ Accepted by QA. If testing fails, move it to ❌ Rejected by QA.

QA Validation Requirements

Before closing a ticket, add validation results in the comments:
  • Scope of testing - What was tested and in which environment
  • Results - Pass/Fail for each scenario
  • Notes / Edge cases - Any noteworthy details, including edge cases covered

Defect Management Process

Defect Creation

Each defect must include:
Summary Short, descriptive title
Steps to Reproduce Numbered, deterministic steps
Expected Result What should happen
Actual Result What happens instead
AttachmentsScreenshots, recordings, logs
EnvironmentQA · Staging · Production
Priority & SeverityBusiness impact & urgency
Impacted ComponentArea of the product affected

Prioritization

🔴 High -> Add to current sprint · Assign directly to developer
🟡 Others -> Discuss during sprint planning with Matt

Production Validation

After deployment:
  1. Retest functionality in Production
  2. Verify the correct merge
  3. Confirm there are no environment-specific issues

Roles & Responsibilities

QA Responsibilities
  1. Validate tickets
  2. Document results
  3. Maintain TestRail
  4. Execute regression
  5. Report defects
  6. Perform production validation
 Development Responsibilities
  1. Provide proof of testing in tickets
  2. Provide impact assessment for complex changes
  3. Merge code to Production

Best Practices

  1. Keep Jira tickets clear and detailed
  2. Ensure all changes are testable
  3. Maintain QA Portal accuracy
  4. Prioritize based on business impact
  5. Always validate critical flows

sections.name: Test Automation
sections.content:
Playwright Automation Repo: https://github.com/simuwatt/automation-qa

Overview

The Buildee QA Automation Framework provides comprehensive end-to-end test automation for the Buildee web application, a building energy management platform, built with Playwright and TypeScript. 

  • Application under test: Buildee (web app)
  • Repository: simuwatt/automation-qa
  • Default environment: Staging (BASE_URL = https://staging.buildee.com)
  • Coverage Matrix: Google Sheets
  • Slack integration for failure notifications

Tech Stack

TechnologyVersionPurpose
Playwright1.56.1Browser automation & test runner
TypeScript5.3.3Programming language
Node.jsLTSRuntime
Faker.js9.9.0Random test data generation
dotenv16.4.7Environment variable management
cross-env7.0.3Cross-platform env variable setting
axios1.8.3HTTP client (TestRail reporting)
csv-parse5.5.6CSV file parsing & validation
pdf-parse1.1.1PDF file content validation
mammoth1.8.0DOCX file content validation
ESLint8.56.0Code linting
Prettier3.2.5Code formatting

Project Structure

The repository is organized by responsibility, with Page Objects, shared fixtures, test data, and utilities kept separate from the test specs.

automation-qa/
├── .github/
│   ├── workflows/
│   │   ├── prod.yml                  # Production smoke tests (scheduled)
│   │   └── staging.yml               # Staging full regression (scheduled)
│   └── scripts/
│       ├── parse_junit_results.sh
│       ├── send_slack_notification_prod.sh
│       ├── send_slack_notification_staging.sh
│       └── report_testrail_results.js
├── data/
│   ├── auth.config.ts                # Auth user definitions
│   ├── buildings.data.ts
│   ├── measures.data.ts
│   ├── json/                         # Test data payloads
│   ├── csv/                          # CSV fixtures & expected headers
│   └── images/                       # Image assets
├── pages/                            # Page Object Model (POM)
│   ├── base/ (BasePage, PortfolioTable)
│   ├── common.page.ts
│   ├── Home/  Headers/  Buildings/  Equipment/
│   ├── Measures/  Proposals/  Reports/  Orgs/
├── tests/
│   ├── auth.setup.ts                 # Runs before all tests
│   ├── base/baseTest.ts              # Extended fixtures & hooks
│   ├── login/                        # Login specs
│   └── e2e/                          # End-to-end specs
├── utils/
│   ├── authHelper.ts
│   ├── NavigationHelper.ts
│   ├── FileUtils.ts
│   ├── calculationsHelper.ts
│   ├── objectModelHelper.ts
│   ├── playwrightHelper.ts
│   └── globalSetup.ts
├── downloads/                        # Temp downloads (auto-cleaned)
├── playwright/.auth/                 # Stored auth state (gitignored)
├── test-results/                     # JUnit XML & screenshots
├── playwright-report/                # HTML reports
├── playwright.config.ts
├── package.json
├── tsconfig.json
├── .env.example
└── .gitignore

Getting Started

Prerequisites

  • Node.js (LTS version recommended)
  • npm (comes with Node.js)
  • Access to the Buildee application (staging or production URL)
  • Valid Buildee user credentials

Installation

# Clone the repository
git clone https://github.com/simuwatt/automation-qa.git
cd automation-qa

# Install dependencies
npm install

# Install Playwright browsers
npx playwright install

Environment Setup

  • Copy the environment template: cp .env.example .env.qa
  • Fill in the credentials in .env.qa:
BASE_URL = https://
USERNAME = 
USEREMAIL = 
PASSWORD =

Environment Configuration

The framework supports multiple environments through dotenv files. The globalSetup.ts module loads the correct .env file based on the test_env variable.

Supported Environment Files

FileEnvironmentUsage
.envDefaultLoaded by default via dotenv.config()
.env.qaQA / StagingUsed with npm run env-qa
.env.stagingStagingUsed with test_env=staging
.env.betaBetaUsed with test_env=beta
.env.productionProductionUsed with test_env=production

All .env files are gitignored and must never be committed with credentials.

Environment Variables Reference

VariableRequiredDescription
BASE_URLYesFull URL of the Buildee app (e.g. https://app.buildee.com)
USERNAMEYesDisplay name shown in the app header (used for assertions)
USEREMAILYesEmail used to log in to Buildee
PASSWORDYesPassword for the login account
test_envNoSelects which .env.<name> file to load (e.g. qa)
CIAutoSet automatically in GitHub Actions; enables retries and forbidOnly

TestRail Variables (optional)

VariableDescription
TESTRAIL_HOSTTestRail instance URL
TESTRAIL_USERNAMETestRail login email
TESTRAIL_PASSWORD / TESTRAIL_API_KEYTestRail credentials
TESTRAIL_PROJECT_IDTestRail project (default: 4)
TESTRAIL_SUITE_IDTestRail suite (default: 6)

Accounts & Credentials

Test Account

The framework uses a single shared test account for all automated tests. The account must have access to the organizations and buildings listed below.

PropertySource
Login emailUSEREMAIL environment variable
PasswordPASSWORD environment variable
Display nameUSERNAME environment variable (used to verify successful login)

Email Account for Report Tests

PropertyValue
Emailtestingaccount@simuwatt.com
PurposeReceives emailed reports during test execution

Authentication Flow

  • Pre-test setup (tests/auth.setup.ts): Before any tests run, the setup project authenticates the user via the browser and saves the session to playwright/.auth/user.json.
  • Session reuse: All chromium project tests load the saved session state, skipping the login step.
  • Login tests: Run in a separate project (Login tests) without stored state, exercising the actual login flow.
  • Re-authentication: If a test detects the login page unexpectedly (session expired), reAuthenticateIfNeeded() in common.page.ts performs an automatic re-login.

GitHub Actions Secrets

Configure the following secrets under Settings > Secrets and variables > Actions:

SecretUsed in
USERNAMEBoth workflows
PASSWORDBoth workflows
USEREMAILBoth workflows
BASE_URLStaging workflow
BASE_URL_PRODProduction workflow
SLACK_WEBHOOK_URLSlack failure notifications
TESTRAIL_URLTestRail reporting (if enabled)
USEREMAIL_TESTRAILTestRail reporting (if enabled)
TRCLI_KEYTestRail API key (if enabled)

Running Tests

NPM Scripts

CommandDescription
npm run testRun all tests with the default .env
npm run env-qaRun all tests using .env.qa (headless)
npm run env-qa-headedRun all tests using .env.qa (headed browser)
npm run env-qa-tagRun tests by tag using .env.qa (headed, append tag)

Playwright CLI Examples

# Run all tests
npx playwright test

# Run a specific spec file
npx playwright test tests/e2e/Buildings/buildingsManagement.spec.ts

# Run tests matching a tag
npx playwright test --grep "@smoke_prod"

# Run all tests EXCEPT smoke production
npx playwright test --grep-invert "@smoke_prod"

# Run in headed mode (visible browser)
npx playwright test --headed

# Run with a specific project
npx playwright test --project=chromium

# Target a custom environment
test_env=staging npx playwright test

Viewing Reports

# Open the HTML report after a test run
npx playwright show-report

Test Architecture

Playwright Configuration Highlights

SettingValueNotes
BrowserChromium (Desktop Chrome)Single browser target
Workers1Serial execution
Viewport1920 x 1080Default; overridden to 1800x900 in beforeEach
Timeout (test)60 seconds
Timeout (expect)9 seconds
Retries0 locally, 3 on CI
ScreenshotsOn failure only
VideoRetained on failure
Test ID attributeidCustom testIdAttribute for getByTestId
CSP bypassEnabledbypassCSP: true

Playwright Projects

ProjectTest DirectoryAuthPurpose
setupMatches *.setup.tsPerforms loginSaves authentication state
chromiumtests/e2e/Uses saved stateMain E2E test suite
Login teststests/login/No stored stateTests the login flow itself
Buildings teststests/Buildings/Uses saved stateIsolated buildings tests (unused path)

Base Test Fixtures (baseTest.ts)

Every test file imports test from baseTest.ts, which extends Playwright's built-in test with:

  • Page Objects: loginPage, homePage, header, dashboard, buildingsPortfolio, buildingPage, overviewTab, panelFilter, utilitiesTab, portfolioTable, projectsTab, newMeasure, newBuildingPage, measuresPortfolio, proposalsPortfolio, proposalsForm, reportsModal, emailFileModal, assetsTab, assetsPortfolio, equipmentForm, locationForm, organizationsDashboard, taskLists, propertyPage
  • Helpers: authHelper, navigationHelper
  • Data: testData (loaded from data/json/testData.json)

Lifecycle Hooks

HookBehavior
beforeEachNavigates to BASE_URL and sets viewport to 1800x900 (skipped for setup/auth tests)
afterEachTakes a full-page screenshot on failure and attaches it as a TestRail annotation
afterAllCleans up the downloads/ directory

Page Object Model

The framework follows the Page Object Model (POM) pattern with a base class hierarchy.

BasePage

The root class providing reusable methods available to all page objects:

  • clickElementWithText(locator, text) — Click an element matching text
  • clickElementWithJS(locator) — Click using JavaScript (for hidden elements)
  • elementContainsText(locator, text, exact) — Assert text content
  • findElementByName(name) — Locate element by name attribute
  • toggleSwitchBox(locator) — Toggle a switch/checkbox
  • dragAndDropElements(source, target) — Drag-and-drop interaction

PortfolioTable

A shared class for interacting with list/table views (portfolios) across the app:

  • clickItem(name) — Click a row by item name
  • verifyItemIsPresent(name) — Assert an item exists in the table
  • verifyItemsArePresent(names[]) — Assert multiple items exist
  • verifyTotalCount(count) — Verify the displayed total
  • clickExtrasButton(name) — Click the extras/action button for an item
  • waitForTableToLoad() — Wait until the table finishes loading

Page Object Inventory

AreaPage Objects
HomeLoginPage, HomePage, DashboardPage
HeadersHeader (org selector, navigation), PanelFilter (search/filter)
BuildingsBuildingsPortfolio, BuildingPage, BuildingForm, OverviewTab, UtilitiesTab, ProjectsTab, AssetsTab, AssetsPortfolio, PropertyPage, LocationForm
EquipmentEquipmentForm (includes OCR and GraphQL response validation)
MeasuresMeasuresForm, MeasuresPortfolio
ProposalsProposalsForm, ProposalsPortfolio
ReportsReportsModal, EmailFileModal
OrganizationsOrganizationsDashboard, TaskLists

Test Data

Required Organizations

KeyOrganization NamePurpose
orgSandboxQA Testing - SandboxMain org for CRUD and regression tests
orgSmokeSmoke TestingUsed by production smoke tests
qaTestingQA TestingGeneral QA testing org

Required Buildings

These buildings must exist and should not be modified manually:

KeyBuilding NameNotes
building1Testing Measure BuildingMeasure-related tests
building2Testing Lighting BuildingLighting equipment tests
building3Testing DO NOT EDITGeneral tests (read-only data)
building4Testing DO NOT EDIT 2General tests (read-only data)
building5Fifth Royal Oak - DO NOT EDITAnalysis workflow tests
building6OCR automated testOCR equipment tests
building7Multiple Lighting EquipmentsBulk lighting equipment tests
oleksandraBuildingOleksandra BuildingAdditional test building

Required Measures

KeyMeasure Name
measure1Testing Measure: Low Flow Faucet Aerators in Commercial/Multifamily buildings
measure2Testing Measure: Lighting Upgrade
measure3Lighting Upgrade (simplified)
measure4Reduce Plug Loads

Required Proposals

KeyValue
TemplateACE Template
Proposal Names"Proposal from proposals tab", "Proposal from measures tab"

Required Equipment

Tests create and delete equipment items during execution. The following equipment types are referenced:

CategoryEquipment TypeExample Tag
WaterStorage TankWater Heater A
HVACAir Handling UnitAir Handler A
HeatingBoiler, Panel, Controller(from equipment JSON files)
CoolingChiller, Pump(from equipment JSON files)
ControlsPanel, VFD, Thermostat(from equipment JSON files)
Air DistributionCAV, DOAS, ERV, VAV, Registers(from equipment JSON files)

CSV Import Files

FilePurpose
data/csv/buildeeUtilityElectricity.csvElectricity utility import
data/csv/buildeeUtilityWaterAndGas.csvWater and gas utility import
data/csv/expectedHeaders.csvValidates CSV export column headers

Equipment JSON Files

Located in data/json/equipment/, these define expected equipment fields for specific types (VAV, Heating, Cooling, Controls, DOAS, etc.).

CI/CD Pipeline

Execution Environments

Tests run in GitHub Actions using official Microsoft Playwright Docker images. Both workflows also support manual triggering via workflow_dispatch.

PipelineDocker ImageSchedule
Production Smokemcr.microsoft.com/playwright:v1.56.0-jammyEvery 3 hours, 8 AM – 7 PM ET, weekdays
Staging Regressionmcr.microsoft.com/playwright:v1.56.1-jammyMonday, Wednesday, Friday at midnight UTC

Production Smoke Pipeline (prod.yml)

  • Schedule: 0 13-23/3,0 * * 1-5 (every 3 hours during US business hours, weekdays)
  • Test filter: --grep "@smoke_prod" (only smoke-tagged tests)
  • Base URL: secrets.BASE_URL_PROD (production environment)
  • Steps: Install deps → Run smoke tests → Upload report → Parse JUnit results → Slack notification (on failures)
  • Retries: 3 (CI mode)

Staging Regression Pipeline (staging.yml)

  • Schedule: 0 0 * * 1,3,5 (Monday/Wednesday/Friday at midnight UTC)
  • Test filter: --grep-invert @smoke_prod (everything except smoke-only tests)
  • Base URL: secrets.BASE_URL (staging environment)
  • Steps: Install deps → Run all non-smoke tests → Upload report → Parse JUnit results → Slack notification
  • TestRail reporting: Present in the workflow but currently commented out

Slack Notifications

Both pipelines send failure notifications to Slack via a webhook. The notification includes:

  • Total / Passed / Failed test counts
  • Names of failed tests
  • Link to the GitHub Actions run

Reporting

Report Types

ReportLocationFormat
HTML Reportplaywright-report/Interactive HTML (open with npx playwright show-report)
JUnit XMLtest-results/junit-report.xmlStandard JUnit XML (used by CI for parsing and TestRail)
List ReporterConsole outputReal-time test progress
Screenshotstest-results/screenshots/PNG, captured on failure
Videostest-results/Retained on failure

Tags & Test Filtering

Tests use tags for selective execution:

TagPurposeExample Usage
@smoke_prodProduction smoke tests--grep "@smoke_prod"
@smokeGeneral smoke tests--grep "@smoke"
@LoginLogin-related tests--grep "@Login"
@issueTests related to known issuesFor tracking
@fixesTests verifying bug fixesFor tracking

Test case IDs (e.g. C315, C1784) are included in test titles for TestRail mapping.

Utility Modules

AuthHelper (utils/authHelper.ts)

Handles programmatic login and session persistence:

  • authenticateUser(username, password) — Logs in via the browser UI
  • saveAuthenticationState(filePath) — Saves cookies/storage to a JSON file

NavigationHelper (utils/NavigationHelper.ts)

Provides common navigation patterns:

  • navigateToBuilding(org, building) — Select org and open a building
  • goToBuilding(building) — Navigate directly to a building
  • navigateToSection(section) — Navigate to a top-level section
  • navigateToBuildingTab(tab) — Switch between building tabs
  • navigateToAssetsSubTab(subTab) — Navigate within the Assets tab

FileUtils (utils/FileUtils.ts)

File handling and validation:

  • readJsonData(path) — Parse a JSON file
  • downloadAndSave(download, fileName) — Save a downloaded file
  • getExpectedHeadersFromCSV(path) — Read expected headers from a CSV
  • validateCSVHeaders(filePath, expected) — Compare CSV headers
  • validatePdf(filePath, expectedText) — Verify PDF content
  • validateDocx(filePath, expectedText) — Verify DOCX content
  • validateDownload(download) — General download validation

PlaywrightHelper (utils/playwrightHelper.ts)

  • withRetry(fn, retries) — Retry wrapper for handling transient failures (page crashes, timeouts)

CalculationsHelper (utils/calculationsHelper.ts)

  • compareValue(actual, expected, tolerance) — Numeric comparison with tolerance
  • reportResults(results) — Aggregate and report comparison results

ObjectModelHelper (utils/objectModelHelper.ts)

  • validateField(page, fieldDef) — Validate a property field against its definition
  • reportFieldValidationResults(results) — Report field validation outcomes

Test Inventory

Login Tests (tests/login/)

SpecTests
login.spec.tsValid login, header validation, dashboard visibility
invalidslogin.spec.tsInvalid credentials, error messages

Smoke Tests (tests/e2e/smokeProd.spec.ts)

Production smoke suite tagged @smoke_prod: navigates through all major tabs, downloads a report.

Building Management (tests/e2e/Buildings/)

SpecTests
buildingsManagement.spec.tsCreate building, archive, restore, building groups (CRUD), column validation
fifthRoyalOak-analysis.spec.tsBuilding analysis workflow on Fifth Royal Oak

Assets Tab (tests/e2e/Buildings/Assets-tab/)

SpecTests
constructions-crud.spec.tsAdd and delete construction items
location-crud.spec.tsAdd, verify, and delete locations
equipment-hvac-crud.spec.tsHVAC equipment CRUD with OCR
heating.spec.tsBoiler, Panel, Controller equipment
cooling.spec.tsChiller, Pump equipment
controls.spec.tsPanel, VFD, Thermostat, etc.
airDistribution.spec.tsCAV, DOAS, ERV, VAV, Registers
lighting-ocr-crud.spec.tsMultiple lighting equipment via OCR
non-lighting-ocr-crud.spec.tsNon-lighting OCR equipment

Overview Tab (tests/e2e/Buildings/Overview-tab/)

SpecTests
overviewTab-utilities.spec.tsUtilities visibility on Overview tab
charts-poc.spec.tsCharts and visualizations
reports-download.spec.tsReport file downloads (PDF, DOCX)
reports-email.spec.tsEmail report functionality

Projects Tab (tests/e2e/Buildings/Projects-tab/)

SpecTests
measure-crud.spec.tsAdd measure to a building
projects-measures-v2.spec.tsV2 measure workflows
proposals-fromBuilding.spec.tsCreate proposal from building context
proposals-crud-from-projects-tab.spec.tsProposal CRUD from Projects tab

Property Tab (tests/e2e/Buildings/Property-tab/)

SpecTests
dataCenter-fields.spec.tsData Center property field validation
adultEducation-fields.spec.tsAdult Education fields (currently skipped)
k12School-fields.spec.tsK-12 School fields (currently skipped)

Utilities Tab (tests/e2e/Buildings/Utilities-tab/)

SpecTests
utilities-electricity-manual.spec.tsManual electricity utility entry
utilities-gas-manual.spec.tsManual natural gas utility entry
utilities-water-manual.spec.tsManual water utility entry
import-water-electricity-gas.spec.tsBulk CSV import of utilities

Measures, Projects, Proposals & Tasks

SpecTests
tests/e2e/Measures/measures-export.spec.tsExport measures data
tests/e2e/Measures/measures-columns.spec.tsMeasure columns and values validation
tests/e2e/Projects/projects-export.spec.tsExport projects data
tests/e2e/Proposals/proposals-crud.spec.tsProposal creation (currently skipped)
tests/e2e/Tasks/taskLists.spec.tsOpen and edit task lists

Troubleshooting

Common Issues

ProblemSolution
Error: browserType.launch: Executable doesn't existRun npx playwright install to download browsers
Tests fail immediately with auth errorsVerify .env credentials are correct; check that BASE_URL is reachable
Session expired mid-testThe framework auto-re-authenticates via reAuthenticateIfNeeded(). If persistent, check account status
Tests pass locally but fail in CICI uses 3 retries. Check if the issue is environment-specific (staging vs production URLs)
Download tests failEnsure the downloads/ directory exists and is writable
USEREMAIL or PASSWORD is emptyEnsure your .env.<env> file is properly filled and test_env matches the file name

Debugging Tips

  • Headed mode: Run with --headed to watch the browser
  • Trace viewer: Uncomment trace: "retain-on-failure" in playwright.config.ts, then use npx playwright show-trace
  • Video replay: Check test-results/ for recorded videos of failed tests
  • Pause on failure: Add await page.pause() in a test to open the Playwright Inspector
  • Single test: Run a specific test with npx playwright test <file> -g "<test name>"

Code Quality

# Run ESLint
npx eslint .
npx eslint . --fix

# Run Prettier
npx prettier . --check
npx prettier . --write

sections.name: Jira Ticket BUIL-12629
sections.content:
Jira Ticket BUIL-12629

The provided source content for this documentation section is limited to a single Jira ticket reference. To create a comprehensive QA documentation section, more detailed information about the feature or topic associated with this ticket would be required.

For direct access to the ticket, please visit: BUIL-12629.
